baseline_prompt_note: v1.1 revised the baseline system_prompt (an editable artifact, not final data) to state the customer is already identified, removing a search-first artifact observed in baseline characterization. Eval splits and their hashes are unchanged from v1.0; heldout and regression_final remain frozen and uninspected.
description: Harder single-step tool-calling scenario with 10 overlapping subscription/billing tools. Target failure family: subscription termination or renewal requests that also carry monetary or prior-charge language, which the baseline tends to misroute toward refund/dispute or to confuse among cancel / disable_auto_renew / pause.
target_failure_family: subscription termination/renewal intent expressed with monetary or prior-charge language; correct tools are cancel_subscription, disable_auto_renew, or pause_subscription

composition.split_procedure: Target-family cases (failure_cluster=money_language_confounds_termination) are placed only in optimize_train/optimize_val/heldout via a deterministic round-robin. Non-target valid behaviors are spread across all five splits with regression weighting; heldout and regression_final were frozen before any optimizer search and were never inspected.
